examTitle: Laws of Motion and Friction Practice Set

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A balloon of gross weight w newton is falling vertically downward with a constant acceleration a (< g). The magnitude of the air resistance is:
questions.explanation: Net force = Weight - Air Resistance => ma = w - R => R = w - ma = w - (w/g)a = w(1 - a/g).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A block of mass m is placed on a smooth plane inclined at an angle θ with the horizontal. The force exerted by the plane on the block has a magnitude:
questions.explanation: The force exerted by the plane on the block is the normal reaction, which balances the perpendicular component of weight: N = mg cos θ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A block of weight 5 N is pushed against a vertical wall by a force 12 N. The coefficient of friction between the wall and block is 0.6. The magnitude of the force exerted by the wall on the block is:
questions.explanation: Normal reaction N = 12 N. Limiting friction = 0.6 * 12 = 7.2 N. Required friction = 5 N (static). Total force by wall = √(N² + f²) = √(12² + 5²) = 13 N.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A block is gently placed on a conveyor belt moving horizontally with constant speed. After t = 4s, the velocity of the block becomes equal to the velocity of the belt. If the coefficient of friction is μ = 0.2, then the velocity of the conveyor belt is:
questions.explanation: The block accelerates due to kinetic friction. a = μg = 0.2 * 10 = 2 m/s². v = u + at = 0 + 2 * 4 = 8 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A force of 98 N is required to just start moving a body of mass 100 kg over ice. The coefficient of static friction is (g = 9.8 m/s²):

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A passenger in a moving train tosses a coin which falls behind him. This shows that the motion of the train is:
questions.explanation: If the coin falls behind him, the train has moved forward relative to the coin's horizontal velocity, implying the train is accelerating.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two blocks of masses 2 kg and 1 kg are placed on a smooth horizontal surface in contact with each other. A horizontal force of 3 N is applied on the 2 kg block. The contact force between the blocks is:
questions.explanation: Acceleration a = F / (m1 + m2) = 3 / 3 = 1 m/s². The contact force pushes the 1 kg block: F_contact = m2 * a = 1 * 1 = 1 N.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A machine gun fires a bullet of mass 40 g with a velocity 1200 m/s. The man holding it can exert a maximum force of 144 N on the gun. How many bullets can he fire per second at the most?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A lift is going up with uniform velocity. The apparent weight of a person in the lift is:
questions.explanation: Since velocity is uniform, acceleration is zero. Hence, apparent weight = true weight.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A heavy uniform chain lies on a horizontal table top. If the coefficient of friction between the chain and the table is 0.25, what is the maximum fraction of the length of the chain that can hang over the edge?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body takes n times as much time to slide down a 45° rough incline as it takes to slide down a 45° smooth incline. The coefficient of kinetic friction is:
questions.explanation: a_smooth = g sin 45°, a_rough = g(sin 45° - μ cos 45°). t = √(2s/a). t_rough / t_smooth = n => a_smooth / a_rough = n² => g sin 45° / g(sin 45° - μ cos 45°) = n² => 1 / (1 - μ) = n² => μ = 1 - 1/n².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A mass of 10 kg is suspended by a string. It is pulled aside by a horizontal force so that the string makes an angle of 60° with the vertical. The tension in the string is (g = 10 m/s²):

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A block rests on a rough inclined plane making an angle of 30° with the horizontal. The coefficient of static friction is 0.8. The frictional force on the block is:
questions.explanation: Since tan 30° (0.577) < μ_s (0.8), the block is at rest. Static friction = applied force = mg sin 30°.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The minimum force required to move a body up an inclined plane is three times the minimum force required to prevent it from sliding down. If the coefficient of friction is μ, what is the angle of inclination?
questions.explanation: Force up F1 = mg(sinθ + μcosθ). Force to prevent down F2 = mg(sinθ - μcosθ). F1 = 3F2 => sinθ + μcosθ = 3sinθ - 3μcosθ => 2sinθ = 4μcosθ => tanθ = 2μ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A particle moves so that its acceleration is always zero. Which of the following must be constant?
questions.explanation: If a = 0, then dv/dt = 0, so velocity is constant. (Momentum is also constant but velocity is the fundamental kinematic quantity).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two masses of 5 kg and 10 kg are connected by a massless string passing over a frictionless pulley. The acceleration of the system is (g = 10 m/s²):

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A rocket of mass 1000 kg is set for vertical firing. If the exhaust speed is 500 m/s, the rate of mass ejection required to just lift the rocket is (g = 10 m/s²):

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body is sliding down a rough inclined plane of angle 30°. If the coefficient of friction is 0.5, the acceleration of the body is (g = 10 m/s²):

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A block of mass 4 kg is kept on a rough horizontal surface with μ = 0.2. A force of 10 N is applied horizontally. What is the acceleration? (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: F_lim = μN = 0.2 * 40 = 8 N. Net force = 10 - 8 = 2 N. a = F_net / m = 2 / 4 = 0.5 m/s².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A wooden box of mass 8 kg slides down an inclined plane of inclination 30° to the horizontal with a constant acceleration of 0.4 m/s². What is the force of friction between the box and inclined plane? (g = 10 m/s²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A uniform rope of length L and mass M is placed on a smooth horizontal surface. A force F is applied at one end. The tension in the rope at a distance x from the applied force is:
questions.explanation: Acceleration a = F/M. Mass of remaining part = M(L-x)/L. Tension = mass * a = (M(L-x)/L) * (F/M) = F(1 - x/L).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: When a horse pulls a wagon, the force that causes the horse to move forward is the force exerted by:
questions.explanation: According to Newton's Third Law, the horse pushes the ground backward, and the ground pushes the horse forward.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A shell of mass M, moving with velocity v, suddenly breaks into two pieces. The part having mass M/3 remains stationary. The velocity of the other part will be:
questions.explanation: By conservation of momentum: Mv = (M/3)(0) + (2M/3)V' => Mv = 2M V' / 3 => V' = 3v/2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A monkey of mass 20 kg is holding a vertical rope. The rope will not break when a mass of 25 kg is suspended from it but will break if the mass exceeds 25 kg. What is the maximum acceleration with which the monkey can climb up along the rope? (g = 10 m/s²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A man of weight mg is moving up in a rocket with acceleration 4g. The apparent weight of the man in the rocket is:
questions.explanation: Apparent weight W' = m(g + a) = m(g + 4g) = 5mg.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If the angle of friction is 30°, then the coefficient of static friction is:
questions.explanation: μ_s = tan θ = tan 30° = 1/√3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A block is kept on a frictionless inclined surface with angle of inclination α. The incline is given an acceleration a to keep the block stationary. Then a is equal to:
questions.explanation: Pseudo force balances gravity component along the plane: ma cos α = mg sin α => a = g tan α.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A coin is dropped in a lift. It takes time t1 to reach the floor when lift is stationary. It takes time t2 when lift is moving up with constant acceleration. Then:
questions.explanation: When lift is moving up with acceleration, effective gravity g' = g + a, which is greater than g. Since t = √(2h/g'), t2 < t1 or t1 > t2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Which of the following forces is not a non-conservative force?
questions.explanation: Gravitational force is a conservative force.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In a tug of war, two teams pull on a rope with equal and opposite forces of 1000 N. The tension in the rope is:
questions.explanation: The tension is equal to the force applied at one end when the system is in equilibrium, which is 1000 N.
